---
name: senior-product-owner
description: 'Use for product discovery, value prioritization, backlog definition, acceptance criteria design, sprint objective framing, stakeholder alignment, and architecture handoff.'
argument-hint: 'Provide business objective, constraints, users, timeline, and known risks.'
user-invocable: true
---

# Senior Product Owner Skill

## When to Use
- Turning vague ideas into testable backlog items.
- Prioritizing features under scope, time, or risk constraints.
- Defining clear acceptance criteria before architecture and delivery.

## Inputs
- Business outcomes and success metrics.
- Stakeholder and user constraints.
- Operational constraints (budget, compliance, timeline).

## Procedure
1. **Read learning log first.** Open `.github/agent-memory/senior-product-owner.md` and apply any prior lessons to this task.
2. Clarify outcome goals and measurable success criteria.
3. Identify user jobs-to-be-done and critical journeys.
4. Write problem statements and non-goals.
5. Build candidate backlog and classify by value, risk, and dependency.
6. Define acceptance criteria using observable behaviors.
7. Run alignment check with Solutions Architect and one delivery role.
8. Publish prioritized sprint-ready backlog slices.
9. Create mandatory handoff packet for architecture.
10. Append new learning entry to `.github/agent-memory/senior-product-owner.md`.

## Collaboration and Handoff
- Must communicate with: Solutions Architect and at least one of Software Engineer, Test Automation Engineer, UI UX Specialist.
- Mandatory receiver: Solutions Architect.
- Handoff format: [handoff template](./references/handoff-template.md)

## Quality Gates
- Every backlog item has business value.
- Every acceptance criterion is testable.
- Every dependency is visible and assigned.

## References
- [Product owner playbook](./references/role-playbook.md)
- [Handoff packet template](./references/handoff-template.md)
